$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1980s (turbo-age sports-desk voice: brash, punchy, headline-driven, big TV money and monster qualifying boost), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Bodies under 'race.result|cause' compose fact tokens and {pool:name} references into 'pools'; champLine is appended to non-DNF bodies only. Allowed fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add eras/events by adding JSON only.

1980s: TRACKSIDE AT {race}, {year}. The wire's red-hot.
SPORTS DESK — {race}, round {round}.
LIVE FROM {race}: cameras rolling, boost climbing.
FROM THE PIT WALL, {race}, {year}.
PADDOCK REPORT — {race}, and the turbos are screaming.
HOT OFF THE WIRE — {race}, {year}, and the boost gauges are pinned.
GRANDSTAND VIEW, {race}: full grid, full song, round {round}.
TELETYPE FROM {race} — {year}, and the turbos won't quit.
SATELLITE FEED, {race}: the world's tuning in, round {round}.
PRESS ROOM, {race}, {year} — typewriters hammering, deadline looming.
STARTER'S ORDERS AT {race} — round {round}, and the wick's already up.
Crank the boost and let them chase — this one's in the book.
The sponsors will love the column inches. Cue the champagne.
Turn it up: nobody's touching {team} in this mood.
One for the highlight reel, and there'll be plenty more.
The TV gantry got its money's worth this afternoon.
Frame it, sell it, bank it — {team} won't stop grinning.
Wind it up again next week; on this evidence nobody's safe.
That's back-page gold, and the boardroom knows it.
Champagne on ice, boost still warm — vintage {team}.
The sponsors just got their money's worth and then some.
Roll the tape one more time; days like this pay the bills.
Kill the boost. The team wants this weekend forgotten.
Back to the drawing board, and fast.
The wick's turned down, and the mood in the garage with it.
No highlights, no headlines, just a long trip home.
The sponsors will want a word before the next round.
Pull the sheets, torch the notes, start again Monday.
A weekend to shred and never mention again.
The garage goes quiet, the boost goes cold, and nobody's smiling.
Load up, ship out, and pray the next one's kinder to {team}.
The debrief will be long, loud and short on laughs.
Not a frame worth keeping, and the sponsors know it.
In the title race {player} sits {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The championship table now reads {player} {champPosition} — {champMove} on the year.
{player} climbs to {champPosition} in the points, {champMove}.
That's {player} {champPosition} in the standings, {champMove}.
Over on the title sheet {player} holds {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The points board says {player} {champPosition} now, {champMove} on the season.
That shuffles the standings: {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}.
In the championship hunt {player} is {champPosition}, {champMove} after that one.
The title arithmetic leaves {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}.
default: That leaves {player} {champPosition} in the title race.

1980s: from a car the form guide had written off for {expected}
against a grid the pundits reckoned good only for {expected}
when the paperwork had pencilled in {expected} at best
when the smart money had them down for {expected}
off a weekend the tipsters marked at {expected}
from grid slots the experts said were worth {expected} and no more
with the form book insisting on {expected}
though every column inch had predicted {expected}
The turbo did the talking.
A thousand horsepower, pointed straight.
Boost to the limit and no apologies.
The wick was wound up all afternoon.
Full song, full commitment from the turbo.
Boost pinned, throttle wide, no lifting.
The turbo sang all afternoon and never missed a note.
Horsepower to spare and the nerve to use it.
Wound to the stop and held there.
The wastegate stayed shut and the results followed.
Turbo lag? Not today — just shove and go.
Millions watched it live.
Prime-time, coast to coast.
The cameras drank it in.
The highlight reel writes itself.
The networks got their money-shot.
Living rooms across the country saw every yard of it.
One for the six o'clock bulletins.
The gantry cameras couldn't get enough.
Prime-time gold, start to finish.
The wire services lit up before the flag dropped.
Roll the season reel, cue the champagne — the boost cools now till spring.
The TV money's counted and the trophies polished; the winter belongs to next year's turbo.
Kill the boost and load the transporters — another turbo-age year goes in the book for {team}.
Cut the feed, chill the fizz — the turbos sleep now and {team} counts the winter down to spring.
Trophies in the cabinet, boost gauges at rest; another loud year done for {team}.
The transporters roll home, the TV cheques clear, and {team} already dreams of next year's monster qualifying boost.
Lights out on {year}: cover the cars, bank the reels, and let the turbo cool till the new season.
Season's a wrap — the highlight tapes go to the vault and {team} points the crew at winter.
default: The books close on another year.
